diagrams.title: Inner Product Geometry
diagrams.description: Shows two vectors u and v with angle θ between them, illustrating how the inner product relates to projection
elements: Perpendicular dropped from u tip to v
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the relationship ⟨u,v⟩ = ||u|| ||v|| cos θ as the projection of one vector onto another scaled by length

diagrams.title: Orthogonal Decomposition
diagrams.description: Shows vector v decomposed into parallel and perpendicular components relative to vector u
elements: Component perpendicular to u: v - proj_u(v)
diagrams.purpose: Understand how any vector can be split into orthogonal components; key to Gram-Schmidt process

diagrams.title: Cauchy-Schwarz Bounds
diagrams.description: Shows the extreme cases: parallel vectors (maximum |⟨u,v⟩|) and orthogonal vectors (minimum, zero)
elements: Case 1: u and v pointing same direction → maximum
Case 2: u and v perpendicular → minimum (zero)
Case 3: u and v pointing opposite → negative maximum
diagrams.purpose: Visualize when equality in Cauchy-Schwarz inequality occurs and what orthogonal means geometrically

diagrams.title: Gram-Schmidt Process
diagrams.description: Step-by-step visualization of orthogonalizing three non-orthogonal vectors in ℝ³
elements: Second orthogonal vector w₂ = v₂ - projection onto w₁
Third orthogonal vector w₃ = v₃ - projections onto w₁,w₂
Right angle symbols between final orthogonal vectors
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how each new vector is made orthogonal by subtracting its projections onto previous ones
